mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the GDP growth rate signals a strengthening economy that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in late-cycle expansion periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1400k signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production above 105 signals a strengthening manufacturing sector that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and a 1-month decrease in the consumer price index below 320 signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs and reduces inflation pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment above 160k signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in retail sales above 720k signals a strengthening consumer sector that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
